# 2026SwerveBot – Copilot Instructions

FRC Team 7165 (Perkins Pirate Robotics) swerve-drive robot for the 2026 season using WPILib 2026 + GradleRIO.

## Build & Deploy

```bash
./gradlew build        # compile and check
./gradlew test         # run JUnit Jupiter 5 tests
./gradlew deploy       # deploy to RoboRIO over USB/Wi-Fi
```

Java 17 required. The fat JAR includes all vendordep JARs (Phoenix 6, REVLib, WPILib).

> **Do not edit `src/main/java/frc/robot/generated/TunerConstants.java` manually.** It is auto-generated by Tuner X. Regenerate via the tuner-project.json if swerve constants need changing.

## Architecture

```
Robot.java (TimedRobot, 20 ms loop)
└── RobotContainer.java
    ├── Subsystems (7 final fields, instantiated in constructor)
    ├── CommandXboxController – primary (port 0), secondary (port 1)
    └── configureBindings() – all button/trigger mappings live here
```

- `CommandScheduler.getInstance().run()` is called every `robotPeriodic()`.
- Autonomous command is fetched via `RobotContainer.getAutonomousCommand()`.
- Phoenix `HootAutoReplay` provides log replay; logs write to `./logs/example.hoot`.

## Subsystem Conventions

Use `codeTemplates/TemplateSubsystem.java` as the starting point for new subsystems.

- Extend `SubsystemBase`; place in `src/main/java/frc/robot/subsystems/`
- Declare all hardware (motors, encoders) as `private final` fields; initialize in constructor
- Expose behavior via **verb-named public methods** (`shoot()`, `intake()`, `move()`)
- Publish real-time telemetry to `SmartDashboard` inside `@Override periodic()`
- Override `simulationPeriodic()` even if empty (required stub)
- Motor drivers:
  - **REV SparkFlex** (brushless) – shooter, intake, bump, suck, turner, wall bed
  - **CTRE TalonFX** (integrated encoder) – swerve drive & steer motors via `TunerConstants`

## Command Conventions

Use `codeTemplates/TemplateCommand.java` as the starting point for new commands.

- Extend `Command`; place in `src/main/java/frc/robot/commands/`
- Constructor: accept subsystem(s) as parameters and call `addRequirements(...)`
- Lifecycle:
  - `initialize()` – one-time setup when scheduled
  - `execute()` – called every 20 ms; invoke subsystem methods here
  - `end(boolean interrupted)` – cleanup (e.g., reset motor speed to 0)
  - `isFinished()` – return `false` for button-held commands, `true` for auto-complete
- Command class names are **PascalCase verbs** (`Shoot`, `Intake`, `AlignToFunnel`)

## Constants

All constants live in `Constants.java` as **inner static classes per subsystem domain**:

```java
public final class Constants {
    public static final class ShooterConstants { ... }
    public static final class BumpConstants   { ... }
    public static final class DriveConstants  { ... }
    // etc.
}
```

- Motor CAN IDs: Shooter=1, Bump=2, Suck=3, Intake=5, WallBed=6/7, Turner=9
- Speed values named with direction or units (`shooterForwardSpeed`, `bumpForwardSpeed`)
- PID constants for autonomous commands go in domain-specific inner classes (e.g., `AlignToFunnelConstants`)

## Units

Use WPILib's `edu.wpi.first.units.Units` static imports:

```java
import static edu.wpi.first.units.Units.*;
// e.g., RotationsPerSecond.of(0.75).in(RadiansPerSecond)
```

Geometry distances use inches (wheel base = ±10.6875").

## Drivetrain

- CTRE Swerve MK4i – 4 modules (FrontLeft, FrontRight, BackLeft, BackRight)
- Max speed: `TunerConstants.kSpeedAt12Volts` (3.79 m/s); max angular: 0.75 rot/s
- Deadband: 10% on drive and rotation axes
- Three drive modes: field-centric (default), robot-centric, brake
- Drive multiplier = 1.0, strafe = 0.3, rotation = 1.0 (see `DriveConstants`)

## Utilities

`Utilities.java` contains a `MathUtilities` inner class with `clamp(value, min, max)` overloads for `double` and `int`. Add general-purpose math helpers here.
